blocks:
Sūrat al-Ṭāriq

Makkiyyah (Meccan), its number of verses is sixteen verses without dispute. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالسَّماءِ وَالطَّارِقِ ** (By the heaven and the night-comer) The star (الكوكب) which appears at night (باللّيل).

(٢) وَما أَدْرَاكَ مَا الطَّارِقُ. (And what will show you what the night-comer is?)

(٣) النَّجْمُ الثَّاقِبُ ** (The piercing star) The luminous one (المضيء), as if it pierces the celestial spheres (الأفلاك) with its light, penetrating them. Al-Qummi said: The الطَّارِقُ is the النَّجْمُ الثَّاقِبُ (the piercing star), and it is the star of punishment (نجم العذاب) and the star of the Resurrection (نجم القيامة), and it is Saturn (زحل) in the highest of stations (على المنازل). In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said to a man from the people of Yemen: "What is Saturn (زحل) among the stars to you?" The Yemeni said: "A star of misfortune (نجم نحس)." He (peace be upon him) said: "Do not say this, for it is the star of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and it is the star of the Successors (الأوصياء), and it is النَّجْمُ الثَّاقِبُ ** (the piercing star) which Allah mentioned in His Book." The Yemeni then asked him: "What does He mean by 'the piercing' (بالثاقب)?" He replied: "Because its rising place (مطلعه) is in the seventh heaven (السماء السابعة), and it pierced (ثقب) with its light until it illuminated the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا). For this reason, Allah named it the النَّجْمُ الثَّاقِبُ (the piercing star)."

(٤) ** إِنْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ لَمَّا عَلَيْها حافِظٌ ** (4) There is no soul but has a keeper over it.

(It is) the answer to the oath, meaning that the matter is that every soul certainly has a watchful keeper (حافظ رقيب) over it. If in (إنْ) is the lightened form [of inna], then the lām is the separating lām (الفاصلة), and mā (ما) is extra (مزيدة). And if lamma (لَمَّا) is read with tashdid, then it is in the meaning of illā (إلَّا), and in (إنْ) is negative (نافية).

Al-Qummi regarding حافِظٌ ** (keeper) said: The angels (الملائكة).

(٥) فَلْيَنْظُرِ الْإِنْسانُ مِمَّ خُلِقَ ** (5) Let man consider what he was created from. So that he may know the truth (صِحَّة) of his return (resurrection), and thus dictate to his guardian (angel) only that which benefits him in his ultimate outcome (عَاقِبَتَهُ). (٦) خُلِقَ مِنْ ماءٍ دافِقٍ ** (6) He was created from a gushing fluid, الدّفق (the gushing) is a pouring (صَبّ) that contains a thrust (دَفْع). Al-Qummi said: It is the semen (النطفة) that exits with force (بِقُوَّة). (٧) يَخْرُجُ مِنْ بَيْنِ الصُّلْبِ وَالتَّرائِبِ ** (7) issuing from between the loins and the breastbones. Between the loins (صُلْب) of the man and the breastbones (تَرائِب) of the woman, which is her chest (صَدْرُهَا). (٨) إِنَّهُ ** (8) Indeed, He is able to bring him back. Meaning, the Creator (الخالق), and His creation (خَلْق) proves this. He said: Just as He created him from semen (نُطْفَة), He is able to return him to the world (الدنيا) and to the Resurrection (القيامة). (٩) يَوْمَ تُبْلَى السَّرائِرُ ** (9) The day when the secrets are examined. They are tested (تُخْتَبَر), recognized (تَتَعَرَّف), and distinguished (تَتَمَيَّز) between what is good (طَابَ) among them and what is evil (خَبُثَ). Al-Qummi said: They are uncovered (يُكْشَفُ عَنْهَا). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, that He was asked what these السَّرائِرُ ** (secrets) are with which Allah tests the servants in the Hereafter (الآخرة). He said: "Your secrets (سَرَائِرُكُمْ) are your deeds (أَعْمَالُكُمْ), including prayer (الصلاة), fasting (الصيام), charity (الزكاة), ablution (الوضوء), the ritual bath (الغسل) from ritual impurity (الجنابة), and every obligatory duty (مَفْرُوض), because all deeds are hidden secrets (سَرَائِر خَفِيَّة). For if a man wishes, he can say, 'I prayed,' when he did not pray, and if he wishes, he can say, 'I performed ablution (تَوَضَّأْتُ),' when he did not perform ablution (يَتَوَضَّأْ). That is His saying: يَوْمَ تُبْلَى السَّرائِرُ."

(١٠) فَما لَهُ فما للإنسان مِنْ قُوَّةٍ وَلا ناصِرٍ. (10) then he has neither power nor helper.

In al-Qummi, narrated from Abu Basir, he said: فَما لَهُ مِنْ قُوَّةٍ ** (then he has neither power) by which he can overcome his Creator وَلا ناصِرٍ ** (nor helper) from Allah who can help him if He intends evil for him.

(١١) وَالسَّماءِ ذاتِ الرَّجْعِ ** (11) By the heaven with its returning [rain], It is said that it returns in every cycle to the place from which it moved. And al-Qummi said: [It means] possessing the rain (المطر). It is said that rain (المطر) was only named raj'an (رَجْعاً) and awban (أَوْباً) because Allah returns it time after time.

(١٢) وَالْأَرْضِ ذاتِ الصَّدْعِ ** (12) and the earth with its cleaving, He said: [It means] possessing the vegetation (النبات).

أقول : Meaning, it cleaves open (تَتَصَدَّع) with vegetation (النبات) and splits open with springs (العيون).

(١٣) إِنَّهُ لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ. (13) this is indeed a conclusive word,

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means that the Quran separates (يَفْصِل) between the truth (الحقّ) and the falsehood (الباطل) by clarifying each one of them.

(١٤) وَما هُوَ بِالْهَزْلِ ** (14) and it is not a jest. For it is entirely serious (جِدّ).

**(١٥) إِنَّهُمْ يَكِيدُونَ كَيْداً ** (15) Indeed, they are devising a stratagem, In nullifying it and extinguishing its light.

(١٦) وَأَكِيدُ كَيْداً ** (16) and I am devising a stratagem. And I will counter them with My stratagem (كَيْدِي) by gradually leading them on (اسْتِدْرَاجِهِم) and taking vengeance upon them in a way they do not anticipate. فَمَهِّلِ الْكافِرِينَ ** (17) So respite the faithless; So do not occupy yourself with taking vengeance upon them, nor hasten their destruction. أَمْهِلْهُمْ رُوَيْداً (respite them for a while) a slight respite (إِمْهَالاً يَسِيراً). Al-Qummi said: Leave them for a little while.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites السماء والطّارق (The Heaven and the Night-Comer) in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه), he will have status (جَاه) and rank (مَنْزِلَة) with Allah on the Day of Resurrection, and he will be among the companions of the Prophets (peace be upon them) and their associates in Paradise (الجنّة).
